narrative:
Florian Wirtz is Germany's standout attacking midfield talent at this World Cup. He holds an impressive overall rating of 7.33/10. This places him in the top 2% of all attacking midfields participating in the tournament.

His creative impact is evident from 0.711 assists per 90 minutes and 2.846 key passes per 90 minutes at this World Cup. Wirtz also contributes defensively, making 1.779 tackles per 90 minutes. However, his direct goal threat is lower, with 0.711 shots per 90 minutes and 0.25 expected goals (xG) per 90 minutes.

Wirtz clearly functions as a central creative force for the German national team, having played 253 minutes. His consistent performances are reflected in a rating range of 6.94–7.72. Scouts and fans should keenly observe his continued elite chance creation and whether he adds more personal goal-scoring impetus in the remaining matches.
